Politics.headline: Trump Fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, Names Mullin as Replacement
Politics.detail: President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and nominated Republican Sen. Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma as her replacement. The reason for Noem's dismissal was not immediately clear.

Politics.headline: US States Sue to Stop Trump's Latest Global Tariffs
Politics.detail: Multiple US states have filed lawsuits to block President Trump's newest round of global tariffs. The lawsuits argue that the tariffs will harm state economies and consumers.

Politics.headline: China Expresses Concern over Middle East Tensions, Urges End to Military Operations
Politics.detail: China's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning stated that China is deeply concerned about the tense situation in the Middle East and urges all parties to stop military operations immediately. China emphasized the need to prevent the conflict from spreading and avoid further escalation.
Politics.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China

Politics.headline: Prime Minister Carney inks agreement with Japanese counterpart to expand trade and defence ties
Politics.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney inked a series of agreements with his Japanese counterpart Friday designed to expand and “modernize” the bilateral relationship as he continues his push to develop deeper ties to like-minded countries in the Indo-Pacific.

Politics.headline: Leak from secret UK meeting about US attacks on Iran a travesty, says Lammy
Politics.detail: David Lammy says leak from secret UK meeting about US attacks on Iran a travesty. The UK is also sending more fighter jets to the Middle East.

Finance & Economy.headline: Mortgage rates rise to 6% as Iran war hits housing market
Finance & Economy.detail: Mortgage rates are rising as bond investors fret that rising oil prices could boost inflation, impacted by the ongoing war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: US states sue to stop Trump's latest global tariffs
Finance & Economy.detail: A lawsuit has been filed by US states to stop the 10% tariffs that President Trump immediately imposed after the US Supreme Court struck down previous tariffs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Manchester United say 5% season-ticket price increase needed to 'return to top of game'
Finance & Economy.detail: Manchester United announced a 5% increase in season-ticket prices to help the team return to the top of their game.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans tap retirement funds at record rates as mounting financial stress takes toll
Finance & Economy.detail: A record 6% of Americans tapped into their 401k savings for hardship withdrawals in 2025, up from 4.8% in 2024, as financial stress forces workers to raid their nest eggs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Italy wins claim over name of Spanish restaurant chain The Mafia Sits at the Table
Finance & Economy.detail: Italy has won a legal claim regarding the name of a Spanish restaurant chain called 'The Mafia Sits at the Table'.

Technology.headline: Autonomous driving firm Oxa raises £77m to scale industrial mobility technology
Technology.detail: Oxford-based autonomous vehicle technology company Oxa has secured $103m (£77m) in Series D funding to accelerate the commercialisation of its self-driving technology for industrial applications, targeting AI-powered software that automates repetitive driving tasks in sectors such as logistics and energy.

Technology.headline: Pentagon labels AI company Anthropic a supply chain risk
Technology.detail: The U.S. military has formally designated artificial intelligence firm Anthropic a supply chain risk, a sweeping move that could cut it off from military contracts. The Trump administration is following through with its threat to designate artificial intelligence company Anthropic as a supply chain risk in an unprecedented move that could force other government contractors to stop using the AI chatbot Claude.

Technology.headline: Hong Kong Tech "Proposed with a Ring" and Won Third Place at MedTech Innovation World Cup 2026
Technology.detail: Hong Kong Science and Technology Parks Corporation (HKSTP) congratulates United Sensing & MediTech for winning third place at the MedTech Innovation World Cup (IWC) with a smart ring that uses AI for personalized and continuous hypertension management. The smart ring tracks indicators and alerts the user to the possibility of worsening conditions with ease and efficiency.

Technology.headline: Tech companies vie for influence over Kansas app store age verification legislation
Technology.detail: A multitude of tech sector lobbyists engaged with a Kansas committee regarding a bill requiring age verification in app stores, with Apple and Google reportedly lobbying committee members extensively. The bill addresses content consumption among minors, and Kansas is among the first states to attempt to pass such a law.

Technology.headline: Block to cut 4,000 jobs, blaming AI
Technology.detail: Fintech company Block said Thursday that it’s cutting more than 4,000 workers or nearly half of its workforce, as artificial intelligence disrupts the way people work. Block blamed AI, saying the powerful technology means it no longer needs as many people.

Science.headline: AI Blood Test Finds Silent Liver Disease Years Before Symptoms
Science.detail: Researchers created an AI-driven liquid biopsy that scans patterns in fragments of DNA circulating in the blood, detecting early liver fibrosis and cirrhosis often unnoticed until serious damage occurs.

Science.headline: Electrons Catapult Across Solar Materials in Just 18 Femtoseconds
Science.detail: Electrons in solar materials can be launched across molecules almost as fast as nature allows, thanks to tiny atomic vibrations acting like a “molecular catapult,” in experiments lasting just 18 femtoseconds.

Science.headline: Advanced 3D reconstructions of the comb jelly's aboral organ reveal a sensory system far more complex than scientists expected
Science.detail: The organ contains a wide variety of specialized cells and is closely linked to the animal's nerve network, allowing scientists to see a sensory system far more complex than they expected.

Science.headline: It's Official: NASA Confirms Asteroid 2024 YR4 Will Fly Past The Moon
Science.detail: The "city killer" asteroid 2024 YR4 won't hit Earth or the moon when it whizzes by in 2032, the latest James Webb Space Telescope observations confirm.

Science.headline: A koala population's rapid rebound may let it escape inbreeding's perils
Science.detail: A recent study shows that a koala population's rapid rebound may allow it to escape the perils of inbreeding.

Health.headline: SOUL: Oral Semaglutide Benefits Extend to Heart Failure
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that oral semaglutide provides benefits beyond diabetes management, showing promise in treating heart failure.

Health.headline: Asthma History Signals Higher Risk for Joint Replacement
Health.detail: Research suggests that individuals with a history of asthma may face a higher risk of needing joint replacement surgery later in life.

Health.headline: Mindfulness Therapy Tied to Fewer Auditory Hallucinations
Health.detail: A study has found a connection between mindfulness therapy and a reduction in the frequency of auditory hallucinations in certain individuals.

Health.headline: CEM Matches MRI for Nipple-Areola Analysis in Breast Cancer
Health.detail: Contrast-enhanced mammography (CEM) shows similar accuracy to MRI in analyzing the nipple-areola complex for breast cancer, according to new research.

Health.headline: Risk Factors for Childbirth-Related PTSS Vary in Parents
Health.detail: A new study reveals that the risk factors associated with post-traumatic stress symptoms (PTSS) following childbirth differ between mothers and fathers.

Sports.headline: India One Win Away From Making T20 World Cup History After Edging Past England
Sports.detail: India is one win away from making T20 World Cup history after narrowly defeating England in a high-scoring semi-final match.

Sports.headline: Women's Asian Cup: India Face Formidable Japan After Gritty Show vs Vietnam
Sports.detail: After a hard-fought victory against Vietnam, the Indian women's team is set to face a strong Japanese side in the Women's Asian Cup.

Sports.headline: Palace hand Spurs FIFTH straight defeat as Van de Ven sees red
Sports.detail: Crystal Palace defeated Tottenham Hotspur, marking Tottenham's fifth consecutive defeat, with Van de Ven receiving a red card during the match.

Sports.headline: KBL: Kevin Quiambao, Goyang edge Rhenz Abando, Anyang
Sports.detail: Kevin Quiambao and Goyang narrowly defeated Rhenz Abando and Anyang in a recent KBL (Korean Basketball League) game.

Sports.headline: Filipinas stumble vs South Korea, face must-win vs Iran at Asian Cup
Sports.detail: The Filipinas team faced a setback against South Korea and now have a crucial must-win game against Iran in the Asian Cup.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears arrested on suspicion of DUI in California
Entertainment.detail: Singer Britney Spears was arrested Wednesday night in Ventura County, California, on suspicion of driving under the influence, the California Highway Patrol has confirmed. She was later released.

Entertainment.headline: Cannes Film Festival to Honor Peter Jackson
Entertainment.detail: The Cannes Film Festival will present filmmaker Peter Jackson with an honorary Palme d'Or, celebrating his blend of Hollywood blockbusters and auteur films. Jackson called the honor one of the greatest privileges of his career.

Entertainment.headline: Gwyneth Paltrow and Robert Downey Jr. added to Oscars presenter lineup
Entertainment.detail: Gwyneth Paltrow and Robert Downey Jr. will join Chris Evans, Javier Bardem, Maya Rudolph and last year's acting winners as presenters at the next Oscars, with Conan O'Brien hosting the ceremony. This year, “Sinners” leads nominations with a record 16.

Entertainment.headline: Harry Styles returns with new album, announces special Netflix concert
Entertainment.detail: After a four year gap, Harry Styles is returning with new music and a special event for his fans, celebrating the release of his new album with a one night concert. Netflix shared the trailer for 'Harry Styles: One Night in Manchester' on March 5.

Entertainment.headline: The Bride!: Monstrously messy, monstrously happy, monstrously Jessie Buckley
Entertainment.detail: Jessie Buckley stars in Maggie Gyllenhaal’s new film, 'The Bride!', as Frankenstein's bride, in a wild and messy celebration of confusion. The film is described as a mix of musical comedy, melodrama, and feminist manifesto.

Crime & Justice.headline: Austin bar shooting bodycam released as DA makes major call about cops who shot suspected terrorist
Crime & Justice.detail: Bodycam video from the bar shooting in Austin, Texas was released Thursday as the district attorney said police officers who shot and killed the suspect will not face a grand jury.

Crime & Justice.headline: NYC Dept of Education employee arrested, charged with murder of Bronx father
Crime & Justice.detail: NYC education worker Naya Brown has been charged in connection with the killing of a Bronx father as he celebrated his birthday in January.

Crime & Justice.headline: US government contractor accused of stealing $46M arrested on small island, FBI announces
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI and French authorities arrested a former government contractor accused of stealing $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect captured after 3 women found dead in southern Utah
Crime & Justice.detail: Utah homicide investigation continues as authorities arrested a suspect after three women found dead in Wayne County following an intensive manhunt.

Crime & Justice.headline: Turtle Creek Resident Pleads Guilty to Sexual Exploitation of Minors
Crime & Justice.detail: A Turtle Creek resident has pleaded guilty to sexual exploitation of minors and is awaiting sentencing.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today (March 06): Top National, Business News, Sports News, Education News & World News with Weather Updates & Thought of the Day
Education.detail: Covers key national, international, sports, education and general news to help you stay informed about what is happening around the world. Includes updates on CUET PG 2026 exams and IIT Madras launching a PG Diploma in Manufacturing Analytics.

Education.headline: Special education teachers report burnout, uncompensated work time, North Dakota survey shows
Education.detail: A survey of North Dakota special education teachers and paraprofessionals reveals widespread burnout due to increased workload, safety concerns, and low pay. The survey, conducted by North Dakota United, indicates that 86% of respondents feel overwhelmed, and 44% are considering leaving special education.

Education.headline: International education 3.0: why the UK must stop treating students like transit passengers
Education.detail: Amanda Broderick, UEL's vice-chancellor, argues that international education policy must evolve beyond recruitment metrics, with universities acting as key players in global skills and innovation ecosystems. She emphasizes the need for regulatory and funding frameworks to support multinational operating models and a shift in political discourse to recognize the broader role of international students.

Education.headline: News from the world of Education: March 6, 2026
Education.detail: St. Mary's Rehabilitation University was formally established in Hyderabad under the Telangana State Private Universities Act, offering specialised schools in rehabilitation, psychology, and special education. Noida International University organised an Educators Meet 2026 in Bardhaman, West Bengal, to celebrate the contribution of teachers.

Education.headline: Empowering schools with insights: the impact of the Oxford International Standardized Assessments
Education.detail: Oxford International Standardized Assessments, developed collaboratively across OUP, provide reliable, externally benchmarked, end-of-phase tests. These assessments offer schools clear insight into learner progress and combine academic rigor with a low-pressure experience to prepare students for future learning.

Lifestyle.headline: Harry Potter Star Daniel Radcliffe Reveals Dramatic Body Transformation
Lifestyle.detail: Daniel Radcliffe, known for his role in Harry Potter, has revealed a significant body transformation.

Lifestyle.headline: 6 Morning Habits That Can Boost Mental Health, According To US Psychiatrist
Lifestyle.detail: A US psychiatrist shares six morning habits that can improve your mental well-being.

Lifestyle.headline: McDonald's CEO roasted after his tiny first bite of new Big Arch burger goes viral
Lifestyle.detail: McDonald's CEO was ridiculed online after a video of him taking a small bite of the new Big Arch burger went viral.

Lifestyle.headline: 1 in 3 Gen Z men believe 'wife should always obey her husband': Global survey
Lifestyle.detail: A recent global survey revealed that one in three Gen Z men believe a wife should always obey her husband.

Lifestyle.headline: Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Women are sharing the unexpected benefits and liberating feelings associated with choosing to be bald.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: US dragging Kurds into its war with Iran would be a disaster
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US is reportedly considering using Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq to help overthrow the Iranian regime, which could lead to instability in the Middle East. The US should instead pursue diplomatic efforts to end the war.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A better budget process—and a better result
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Harvard's fiscal year 2027 budget process reduced a projected deficit of $1.6 million to between $85,000 and $396,754 without major cuts, due to both good fortune and a collaborative process. The town's employees and volunteer committee members accomplished much through early engagement and public information.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Iran to take US focus from Taiwan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US, along with Israel, launched "Operation Epic Fury" to remove the Iranian supreme leader, which is believed to be a prelude to changing the geopolitical landscape and targeting China's rise. Reports suggest that weakening the Iranian regime would free up US assets to be assigned to the Indo-Pacific region, deterring potential adversaries.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A few bad LPDP apples
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Indonesian government is facing scrutiny regarding its national scholarship program (LPDP) after a recipient's wife flaunted their family's British citizenship, sparking public outrage. Calls are mounting for stricter screening of candidates, including social media checks, to ensure the scholarship is awarded to deserving individuals who represent the country and come from less privileged backgrounds.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: War-making is the president's business, not Congress's
Opinion/Editorial.detail: George F. Will argues that while the executive branch is powerful, especially in war-making, modern circumstances and technologies often strengthen executive unilateralism, making it difficult for Congress to check presidential power. He notes historical examples of presidents marginalizing Congress in military actions and suggests that legal processes are often insufficient to restrain presidential authority in this area.

World.headline: Tehran hit by heavy bombing on day seven of US-Israel war on Iran
World.detail: The conflict is escalating as regional tensions rise after six days of US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

World.headline: More than 120 killed in Israel's Lebanon attacks as Beirut, south, east hit
World.detail: Lebanon's Hezbollah group urges Israelis to evacuate border areas as Israel continues to bomb the country.

World.headline: Iran Ready for U.S. Ground Invasion: FM
World.detail: Tensions escalate as Iran prepares for potential ground invasion by the United States amidst ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Canada's military chief says allies to talk defending Gulf states from Iran
World.detail: A meeting is set for early Friday morning to discuss a proposal among allied militaries regarding defending Gulf states from Iran, with a recommendation to be presented to the government.

World.headline: Moscow sees significant rise in demand for Russian oil, gas amid Iran war
World.detail: Amid the Iran war, Moscow is experiencing a significant increase in demand for Russian oil and gas.

Zimbabwe.headline: Solar power in rural Zimbabwe hasn't reduced women's unpaid work: can policy do better?
Zimbabwe.detail: A recent analysis explores how solar power initiatives in rural Zimbabwe have not significantly reduced the burden of unpaid work on women, suggesting a need for policy adjustments.

Zimbabwe.headline: Crackdown on false prophets, unlicensed doctors
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is intensifying its efforts to eliminate false prophets and unlicensed doctors, signaling a stricter regulatory environment for such practices.

Zimbabwe.headline: Byo councillors sign anti-corruption pledge after earlier resistance
Zimbabwe.detail: Following initial reluctance, councilors in Bulawayo have committed to an anti-corruption pledge, indicating a renewed focus on ethical governance in the city.

Zimbabwe.headline: MPs raise concern over low visibility of Women's Microfinance Bank
Zimbabwe.detail: Members of Parliament are expressing concerns about the limited reach and impact of the Women's Microfinance Bank, suggesting the need for strategies to enhance its visibility and accessibility.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe taking steps to clear $23bn debt, president says
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is actively pursuing strategies to address its substantial $23 billion debt, according to the president.
